data.title: Vale slashes outlook for full-year iron ore agglomerates production
data.description:
Vale (NYSE:VALE) said Wednesday it has lowered guidance for FY 2025 production of iron ore agglomerates, as the pellet market struggles with oversupply concerns and reduced demand for high-quality products.

The Brazilian miner cut its outlook to 31M-35M metric tons, down from a previous range of 38M-42M tons, saying in a securities filing that the decision reflected "current market conditions for pellets," while all other estimates remained unchanged.

Analysts said the downside guidance was not a surprise, as pellet prices have been under pressure and Vale (NYSE:VALE) executives have emphasized that its portfolio is flexible and competitive.

"Vale's decision aligns with the subdued market environment," Wood Mackenzie analyst Artur Bontempo said, adding that compressed steel margins have pushed mills to favor cheaper, lower-grade ore over high-cost pellets.

Market conditions also have been pressured by rising output from the Samarco joint venture between Vale and BHP, which is set to add 8M tons of pellets and pellet feed to the market as it ramps up production.

Vale's (VALE) new guidance would reduce seaborne pellet supply by 7M tons at the midpoint, representing 6% of the total market, according to RBC analysts.

data.title: Ambiq Micro files for IPO, set to trade under AMBQ ticker
data.description:
Semiconductor company Ambiq Micro filed on Thursday for its upcoming initial public offering, and said it will trade under the “AMBQ” ticker on the New York Stock Exchange.

The Austin, TX.-based company generated $15.7M in revenue in the first-quarter of 2025, up from $15.2M in the year-ago period, the company said in its S-1 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Revenue for the 2024 fiscal year was $76.1M, up from $65.5M in fiscal 2023.

Ambiq saw a $14.8M net sales increase in customers from the U.S., Europe and Asia (excluding Mainland China), a 94% increase from the year-ago period. Net sales from the aforementioned group grew to $38.1M in 2024, up 70% year-over-year. The company said it intends to focus its business outside of Mainland China.

Products from the company are inside more than 270M devices (many of them at the edge), including shipping more than 42M in 2024.

“We seek to drive growth in AI adoption at the edge in the personal devices, medical/healthcare, industrial edge, and smart home and building markets and continue to set new standards in edge AI performance and power efficiency,” the company said in the filing. “Over time, we expect to integrate our ultra-low power technology into additional chip products that benefit from greater power efficiency, including high-performance compute applications such as AI data centers and automotive.”

Ambiq competes with a number of large microcontroller companies, including Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN), Microchip (MCHP), STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM), Silicon Laboratories (SLAB), NXP Semiconductors (NASDAQ:NXPI), Renesas and Infineon (OTCQX:IFNNY). It also said it competes with connectivity companies such as Synaptics (SYNA) and other larger semiconductor companies, such as Qualcomm (QCOM). 

The IPO is being led by BofA (BAC) Securities, and other investment banks on the offering include UBS (UBS), Needham and Stifel.

data.title: Amazon consolidates streaming services by folding Freevee into Prime Video
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is shutting down the Freevee standalone app in August amid efforts to consolidate all content under its Prime Video platform, according to a notice on the Freevee app.
  • Freevee was folded into Amazon’s streaming service at its 2019 launch, originally operating under the IMDb brand, which Amazon had acquired in 1998. The company notified viewers in November that it had “decided to phase out Freevee branding,” and would integrate the service into Prime Video.
  • After August, viewers will be able to find Freevee content under the “Watch for Free” section on Prime Video.
  • Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) shares are fractionally higher into Thursday’s open.

data.title: 4 biggest stocks to watch on Thursday: FDX, HOOD, BABA and EPD
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Thursday, as traders turned their attention to key labor data and also awaited more possible trade deals. Here are four stocks to watch out for:

FedEx (FDX) shares were up 1.4% in premarket trade after BNP Paribas flipped from being a bear on the shipping stock to being a bull. BNP Paribas thinks the relative operational outperformance to rival United Parcel Service (UPS) will continue because UPS is facing company-specific headwinds. FDX has been down 15% since the start of the year. 

Robinhood (HOOD) stock fell 2.7% in early trading as OpenAI issued a firm statement Wednesday denying any affiliation with the tokenized equity tokens offered by the trading platform, emphasizing that the tokens do not represent real ownership or equity in the company. HOOD had begun offering tokenized shares of OpenAI and SpaceX to its users in Europe.

Alibaba (BABA) fell 2.4% in premarket trade after it announced plans to raise about HK$12B ($1.53B) through an exchangeable bond offering to boost investments in cloud infrastructure and global commerce operations. The bonds are linked to Alibaba Health Technology, and Alibaba Group holds about 64% of equity interest in Alibaba Health.

Enterprise Products (EPD) stock was up 0.5% in early trading after the U.S. government removed license requirements for shipping ethane to China, clearing the way for deliveries to Chinese ports without additional approvals.

data.title: Bullish sentiment rises, shows recent AAII survey
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors rose for the second consecutive week, showed the recent survey conducted by American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bulls as per the AAII sentiment survey stood at 45% for the week ending July 2, compared to 35.1% in the prior update. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment as per the survey fell to 33.1% for the week, versus 40.3% the week before.

Investors kept their focus on the ongoing trade negotiations during the week. The UK-US trade deal coming into force on Monday, slashing US export tariffs for the UK's automotive and aerospace sectors, reignited hope in the market. The U.S. and China also finalized a trade truce reached last month, with Beijing agreeing to supply rare earths in exchange for the lifting of U.S. countermeasures.

“Tariff-related fears temporarily pressured the stock, but recent negotiations have reduced long-term risk, restoring confidence in the growth story,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis, adding that while the stock has rebounded nicely from the April dip so far into early July, the shares are “still well priced for several years of growth if the US consumer holds up.”

The benchmark S&P500 (SP500) and Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gained over 1% in the last one week, while Dow (DJI) rose over 2%.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also fell during the week, with the figure standing at 21.9%, compared to 24.7% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Baidu shows off search with AI revamp, image-to-video model MuseSteamer
data.description:
Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) confirmed that it has revamped its Search with New AI features in its biggest overhaul in a decade.

The Chinese internet search giant also confirmed the launch of its image-to-video model MuseSteamer.

Baidu said that at the heart of this revamp is the upgraded Smart Search Box that supports long-form queries of over 1,000 Chinese characters, accepts multimedia inputs including image, audio, video, and file, and integrates directly with AI tools for writing, image generation.

 

 

Baidu noted that if a user is planning a travel itinerary or trying to recall a vague movie plot, the Smart Search Box can interpret the request and deliver precise, helpful results.  For example, users can take a photo of a scenic spot for instant tour commentary, snap a faulty appliance for troubleshooting, or upload a medical report for AI-powered interpretation.

Baidu added that Search's upgraded Baikan feature restructures search result pages, going beyond traditional links to deliver structured answers that integrate formats including text, images, videos, charts, among others.

With services like AI agents to be integrated, Baikan provides tailored, actionable information — such as organizing product recommendations and even enabling direct purchases from the search page.

The AI Assistant feature now offers video calling, multimodal input (text, voice, image, and files), and outputs including notes, images, and videos, according to the company.

 

 

Baidu noted that upgraded Baidu Search also features advanced content creation capabilities, offering a one-stop solution from ideation to video generation to publishing.

Users can create a three-minute AI-generated video from a single sentence, with built-in tools for storyboard editing and custom scene design, the company added.

Baidu also confirmed the launch of MuseSteamer, an image-to-video model enabling video generation featuring synchronized visuals, sound effects, and dialogue. Baidu is integrating MuseSteamer into Baidu Search.

The model can simultaneously generate synchronized visuals, speech, and sound effects in Chinese. It can produce 10-second 1080P cinematic-quality clips from just one image — complete with nuanced facial expressions and camera motion.

The MuseSteamer model family includes Turbo, Lite, Pro, and a full lineup of voiced versions. The Turbo version is currently available for limited-time free public testing on Huixiang, Baidu's AI video creation platform, with the other versions set to roll out starting in August, the company added.

Baidu was among the first group of Chinese companies whose AI model was approved in China following the launch of OpenAI's ChatGPT in late 2022 in the U.S. However, the company's Ernie Bot faces fierce competition in the Chinese market, with tech giants like Alibaba (BABA) and startups such as MiniMax, Baichuan and Zhipu AI also introducing AI models.

data.title: Trican Well Service Ltd. raises dividend by 10% to $0.055
data.description:
• Trican Well Service Ltd. (TSX:TCW:CA) declares $0.055/share quarterly dividend, 10% increase from prior dividend of $0.050.
  • Forward yield 4.82%
  • Payable Sept. 30; for shareholders of record Sept. 12; ex-div Sept. 12.
  • See TCW:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Embraer tallies 30% increase in Q2 aircraft deliveries
data.description:
Embraer (NYSE:ERJ) +2.4% pre-market Thursday after saying it delivered 61 aircraft during Q2, a 30% increase from a year earlier, when 47 jets were delivered, and more than double the volume recorded in Q1 2025.

The deliveries in the quarter through the end of June included 19 commercial jets, in line with the same quarter last year, 38 executive jets, a 41% Y/Y jump, and four defense aircraft.

The Brazilian planemaker said it expects to deliver 77-85 commercial planes and 145-155 executive jets this year.

Embraer (NYSE:ERJ) shares hit a fresh all-time high in Sao Paulo on Tuesday as it secured an order from Scandinavian Airline SAS for up to 55 aircraft, in a deal worth ~$4B excluding purchase rights.

The deal will make Scandinavia's biggest carrier one of the largest operators of Embraer's (NYSE:ERJ) E2 family, and analysts said it signals positive demand for the planemaker's next-generation jetliners.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures up sharply as labor report comes above consensus
data.description:
Stock index futures rose sharply, while yields were higher on Thursday as nonfarm payrolls jumped more than expected in June.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.36%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 10 basis points to 3.90%.

June nonfarm payrolls came in at +147K vs. +110K consensus and +144K in May (revised from +139K), while the unemployment rate was 4.1% vs. 4.3% consensus and +4.2% prior.

U.S. stocks ended mostly higher on Wednesday, as a positive trade development overshadowed an unexpected dip in U.S. June private sector employment.

On Wednesday, U.S. President Donald Trump said a trade deal had been reached with Vietnam, under which the U.S. will get “total access” to the Vietnamese market. Vietnam will pay a 20% tariff on goods sent into the U.S.

"It’s been an eventful 24 hours in markets, with a bunch of competing narratives across different asset classes. On the bright side, the S&P 500 hit a new record after the US reached a trade deal with Vietnam, which raised hopes they were about to announce multiple deals before the reciprocal tariff deadline on July 9," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Other economic releases for the day include the initial jobless claims, which is expected to come in at 240K.

The June PMI Composite Final and the June ISM Service Index will land during market hours. The former is expected to come in at 52.8, and the latter at 50.8.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.  

The stock market will close at 1 pm ET today on account of the Friday Independence Day holiday.

data.title: Needham drops its bearish view and moves to the sidelines on Meta Platforms
data.description:
Needham on Thursday took a more neutral stance on social media and tech giant Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and gave it a "hold" from its previously assigned "underperform" rating.

Analyst Laura Martin thinks the rating reflects Meta's slowing labor productivity improvement, driven by rising headcount and higher costs per FTE, which she believes works against share price appreciation.

The research firm, however, expects the company to over-deliver on its prior revenue and margin estimates for Q2 and FY25 and forecasts that it will report 14% revenue growth and 6% EPS growth in 2025.

Needham chose not to turn bullish on Meta as the company's capital budgets continue to rise. It also sees a structural cost disadvantage against rivals like Google (GOOGL) (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), and Microsoft (MSFT), who own their cloud assets, and regulatory risks related to privacy, antitrust, and content moderation, among other reasons.

"We continue to worry about META's rapidly rising CapX budgets, as it tries to catch up with companies much larger than itself in the LLM wars," the research firm said in its July 3 note. "We estimate that META's CapX will be $68B in FY25, up 84% y/y, the fastest growth among the hyperscalers. The ROIC on this CapX is uncertain, and the larger the spending, the more likely there is waste, in our view."

META does not have a price target from Needham at the moment. Stock is up about 22% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index is up nearly 6%.
